_meta.description: Hand-curated knowledge base of MetalBench kernel-optimization patterns. Indexed by bottleneck_class → kernel_kind → list of techniques. Patterns are added by hand when a Verifier-kept attempt produces >=15% improvement. The Optimizer's extract() function queries this store first, then falls back to the profiler's LLM suggestions.

conv.rationale: Invert the conv loop nest so each thread processes ONE spatial position and computes ALL output channels at once. Each input activation is read 9 times (3×3 stencil) instead of 9×C_out times. Activations stay in registers, reused across output-channel FMAs.
anti_patterns: loop reorder may regress M2 if register pressure goes up — cross-chip check required

conv.technique: #pragma unroll + half-precision accumulator on inner channel reduction
conv.rationale: On small-channel convs (C ≤ 16) the inner reduction is short enough to fully unroll. Half-precision accumulator keeps register pressure low while unrolling exposes ILP. Final sum cast back to fp32 before output write.
anti_patterns: fp16 accumulator on attention softmax — drifts beyond rtol

matmul.technique: simdgroup_matrix<float,8,8> MMA tiles for the matmul step
matmul.rationale: Replaces scalar-loop dot products with hardware 8x8 fp32 matrix multiply-accumulate units. Available on all M-series. Up to 8x faster for compute-bound matmuls.
anti_patterns: MMA on undersized matrices (M<8 or N<8) wastes simdgroup units

fused.technique: fuse multi-output inner loop to share LN computation
fused.rationale: When a kernel re-computes LayerNorm inside per-head/per-output loops, hoist it to compute LN once into TG memory or registers and read it across outputs.

elementwise.rationale: Smaller grid means each TG does more work per shared/constant load. When the kernel reads a small parameter (bias, scale) repeatedly, fewer TGs means each load is amortized over more elements.

elementwise.technique: N-row unroll with amortized vector load
elementwise.rationale: Each thread processes N rows and amortizes one float4 load across all rows.

reduction.rationale: Reduce threadgroup to 256 threads to free register pressure, vectorize input loads with float4, finalize via simd_max/simd_sum without a per-thread serial pass.

matmul.rationale: Each simdgroup owns 8 contiguous output columns. Lanes split the K-dim reduction with float4 row loads (contiguous instead of strided). Replaces cross-lane simd_shuffle_xor with simd_sum.

any.rationale: When SOL is sub-5% and neither ceiling is saturated, the kernel is launch/stall-bound. Micro-optimizations (unroll, float4) hit a noise floor. Look at: collapsing barriers, reducing dispatch count, batching small kernels, or restructuring the loop nest entirely. Anti-pattern: any single-line tweak.

any.technique: shrink threadgroup to within PSO max
any.rationale: Silent dispatch failure when threadgroup > PSO max (M2 caps register-heavy kernels at 896). Output stays zero; bench reports nonsense timing. Shrink the TG, smaller tile dimensions.

Multi-input kernels (pred + target for loss kernels) must have explicit input_bindings=(0,1...). The ew() helper assumes single-input; misalignment causes the output buffer to be written into a scalar slot → OOB → silent GPU hang.
